name: CLOCK_c
description: Periodic clock generator — generates periodic events with configurable period
criticalRules: A reference .xcos file EXISTS: call get_xcos_block_example('CLOCK_c') — use it as your template.
ALL top-level exprs/realParameters/integerParameters MUST be ScilabDouble height='0' width='0'. NEVER fill them with values.
MUST contain a <SuperBlockDiagram> child element AFTER <mxGeometry> — NEVER flatten to a plain block.
SuperBlockDiagram internal blocks: EventOutBlock(CLKOUT_f) + BasicBlock(EVTDLY_c) + SplitBlock(CLKSPLIT_f).
Internal block tags: CLKOUT_f uses <EventOutBlock>, CLKSPLIT_f uses <SplitBlock>, EVTDLY_c uses <BasicBlock>.
3 CommandControlLinks in SuperBlockDiagram: EVTDLY_c.cmd->CLKSPLIT_f.ctrl, CLKSPLIT_f.cmd1->CLKOUT_f.ctrl, CLKSPLIT_f.cmd2->EVTDLY_c.ctrl.
Top-level CommandPort: ONE CommandPort sibling of the block (parent=block id), ordering='1', initialState='-1.0'.
SuperBlockDiagram MUST start with <Array as='context' scilabClass='String[]'></Array> and end with <mxCell as='defaultParent' .../>.
anomalies: SuperBlock — NEVER generate as a flat BasicBlock with scalar parameters.
Has exactly ONE CommandPort output at the top level (the clock output).
Period is configured via internal EVTDLY_c exprs — not via top-level params.
Internal feedback loop: CLKSPLIT_f splits event to both CLKOUT_f and back to EVTDLY_c.
